items.title: Iran's strategic options in the conflict with Israel | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Iranian officials have reportedly told mediators they will not negotiate any ceasefire with Israel while under attack. The two have continued to exchange missile fire, with both sides threatening to escalate the conflict further. The Israeli air force says it has launched strikes on dozens of targets across Iran, including the capital Tehran.

items.title: Annual G7 summit set to start in Canada amid Israel-Iran escalation | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The annual G7 summit of leading industrialized nations is set to kick off in Canada, with global security concerns front and center. Talks will focus on the escalating conflict between Israel and Iran, as well as Ukraine, economic resilience and access to critical raw materials.

items.title: Israel's attacks on Iran: Endgame regime change? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel and Iran are attacking each other for a third day with both sides threatening to escalate. Explosions have been reported from the Iranian capital Tehran. Ten Israelis were killed and more than 100 injured in Iranian missile strikes overnight, while a further barrage by Iran on Sunday afternoon was mostly intercepted by Israeli defenses.

00:00 Hans-Jakob Schindler, Counter Extremism Project; Shani Roxanes, DW Middle East Analyst

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Officials in Gaza say dozens more people have been killed in Israeli military operations. Many of the victims died as they waited for food supplies. There's also growing concern about what the hostilities between Israel and Iran could mean for Gaza.

items.title: What is Israel's goal in Iran and why is it attacking oil facilities? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>At least ten people have been killed and more than 100 injured in Israel in retaliation strikes launched by Iran. The Iranian regime has been responding to Israeli attacks on its nuclear sites. In Bat Yam, south of Tel Aviv, a missile hit an apartment block. Rescue teams are searching for dozens of people who are still missing.

00:00 Israel and Iran trade deadly strikes
02:37 DW's Emily Gordine reports from Tel Aviv
07:21 DW speaks with Sara Bazoobandi, a non-resident research fellow at the Institute for Security Policy at the University of Kiel
14:50 DW speaks with Simon Mabon, a Middle East analyst at Lancaster University in the UK

items.title: The political comeback of Pakistan's military | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The military is still being celebrated in Pakistan. Pakistan declared victory after its latest standoff with India. The intense confrontation had brought the South Asian neighbors to the edge of their fifth war, as they launched missiles and drones toward each other over four days in early May. But there is also skepticism around what the new surge of military means for Pakistan's democracy.

items.title: Further escalation: Israel and Iran trade new attacks | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Overnight, Israel and Iran engaged in renewed and intense waves of attacks. Multiple reports from cities including Tel Aviv and Jerusalem confirm that several buildings were hit in the strikes. According to Israeli sources, the Israeli Air Force targeted the Ministry of Defense, infrastructure related to Iran’s nuclear weapons program, and oil depots in the capital Tehran.

items.title: Why Trump's military parade is so controversial | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Huge crowds have taken to the streets of major cities from New York to Los Angeles to protest President Donald Trump. The protests are the largest against Trump since he was re-elected, and came on the day that thousands of troops, military vehicles and aircraft rolled through Washington in an unusual display of American might. The parade was in honor of the US Army's 250th birthday, and it just happened to coincide with the president's 79th.

00:00 Demonstrators rally across US against Trump at 'No Kings' protests
03:28 DW's Steven Beardsley provides analysis

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.author: DW News
items.content:

items.title: Iran cancels next round of nuclear talks with US as Israeli strikes continue | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Israeli military says it is still striking Iran and has hit more than 150 targets in the past 24 hours. Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has threatened a "more severe" response if Israel continues its attacks. Pezeshkian said that Iran would not participate in the next round of negotiations with the US in Oman on Sunday, calling the talks "meaningless" after Israel's strikes against Iranian nuclear sites. 
The Israeli attacks have struck nuclear and military facilities, as well as residential areas in Iran. The strikes killed 20 Iranian military commanders. Iran's UN Ambassador Amir-Saeid Iravani said 78 people including civilians were killed and more than 320 wounded.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Deadly Israeli strikes on Iran continue
0:25 Barbara Slavin, Iran analyst, Distinguished Fellow at the Stimson Center in Washington D.C., on whether US was involved in attacks on Iran

items.title: How close was Iran to reaching nuclear breakout capacity before Israel struck its nuclear sites?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel has been using warplanes to target key facilities, top generals and scientists across Iran. It says it has gained air superiority from the west of Iran all the way to the capital Tehran. Israel maintains its efforts are focused on disabling Tehran's nuclear enrichment program. Yet Israeli missiles struck residential areas in Iran Tehran. At least 130 people have been killed in Iran since Israel began its airstrikes, authorities say. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Israel strikes nuclear sites and residential areas in Iran
0:33 Danny Citrinowicz, Middle East analyst at the Atlantic Council, who formerly served in various Israeli defense intelligence positions, on why Israel decided to strike Iran while US-Iran negotiations were ongoing 
08:10 Nawaf M. Al-Thani, analyst on international relations and defense in Doha, on Iran's retaliatory strikes and possibilities for de-escalation
